audioscript.text: Section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a telephone conversation between a travel agent and a customer.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 6.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example that has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning, World Tours.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: My name is Jamie.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Good morning. I want some information on self-drive tours in the USA.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Could you send me a brochure?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Of course.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Could I have your name please?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Andrea Brown.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: The customer's name is Andrea Brown, so Brown has been written in the space.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 6.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning, World Tours.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: My name is Jamie.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Good morning, I want some information on self-drive tours in the USA.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Could you send me a brochure?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Of course.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Could I have your name please?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Andrea Brown.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Thank you.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: And your address?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: 24 Ardleigh Road.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Can you spell that?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: ARDLEIGH Road.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Post code?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: BH52OP.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Thanks, and can I have your phone number?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Is a mobile all right?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Thank you.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: And can I ask you where you heard about World Tours?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: From a friend, or did you see an advert somewhere?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: No, I read about you in the newspaper.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: OK, I'll get the brochures in the post to you, but can I give you some information over the phone?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Uh. What kinds of things do you want to do on your holiday?
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: I'm interested in going to California with my family.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: I've got two children, and we want to hire a car.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: OK, we have a couple of self-drive tours there, visiting different places of interest in California.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: The first one begins in Los Angeles, and there's plenty of time to visit some of the theme parks there.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Uh. That's something on my children's list, so I'd want to include that.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Good.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Uh. Then you drive to San Francisco, from San Francisco you can drive to Yosemite Park, where you spend a couple of nights.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: You can choose to stay in a lodge or on the campsite.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: I don't like the idea of staying in a tent.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: It would be too hot.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: And the tour ends in Las Vegas.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: OK, the other trip we can arrange is slightly different.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: It starts in San Francisco, then you drive south to Cambria.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Someone told me there's a really nice castle near Cambria.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Will we go near that?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Hearst Castle is on that road, so you could stop there.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Good, I'd like to do that.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Does this trip also go into the desert?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: No, it continues to Santa Monica, where most people like to stop and do some shopping.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: We have enough of that at home, so that doesn't interest us.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Ha ha, OK.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Well you could go straight on to San Diego.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: That's good for beaches, isn't it?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: That's a good place to relax, and your children might like to visit the zoo before flying home.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Uh. I don't think so.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: We want some time for sunbathing and swimming.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 7 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 7 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So, how many days are the trips, and how much do they cost?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: The first one I told you about is a self-drive tour through California, which lasts 12 days and covers 2,020 km.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: The shortest journey is 206 kilometers, and the longest is 632 kilometers.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: The cost is £525 per person.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: That includes accommodation, car rental, and a flight, but no meals.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: OK, and the other trip, that lasts nine days, but you spend only three days on the road.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: You cover about 980 km altogether.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: So is that cheaper then?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: Yes, it's almost £100 cheaper, it's £429 per person, which is a good deal.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: So that covers accommodation and car hire.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: What about flights?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: They aren't included, but these hotels offer dinner in the price.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: OK, well, thank you very much.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: I'll be in touch when I've had a chance to look at the brochure.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: I'm pleased to help.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Goodbye.
audioscript.speaker: TRAVEL AGENT

audioscript.text: Goodbye.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREA

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a fitness manager of a leisure club talking to some new members.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 and 12.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 11 and 12.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On behalf of LP clubs, I'd like to welcome you all here today.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: My name is Sandy Fisher, and I'm one of the fitness managers here.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Before we start our tour of the club, I'll just run through some basic information about the facilities we have here, including recent improvements, and explain the types of membership available.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Our greatest asset is probably our swimming pool, which at 25 meters isn't Olympic sized, but now we've expanded it to eight lanes, it's much wider.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This means there are rarely more than a couple of people at a time in each lane.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Unfortunately, there isn't space for an outdoor pool here, but the glass roof on the swimming pool is partly retractable.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Which means you can enjoy something of the open air experience on warmer days.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Our recently refurbished fitness suite has all the latest exercise equipment, including 10 new running machines.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And a wide range of weight training machines.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Each member is given full training in how to operate the equipment, and there is always a trainer on duty to offer help and advice.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Although we do have adult only times after 6, and at certain times at weekends.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Children are well catered for, older children continue to benefit from a wide range of tuition, anything from trampolining to yoga.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 13 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 13 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: One thing all our members appreciate about us is that we take very good care of them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This starts on day 1 with your personal assessment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You are asked to fill in a questionnaire giving details of any health problems.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: One of our personal trainers will then go through this with you.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The trainer will then take you through the safety rules for using the equipment in the fitness suite.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: During your next exercise session, a personal trainer will work with you to make sure you understand these.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's very important to do this, because we really do want to avoid having any sports injuries.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There's a lot more to looking after yourself than simply lifting weights.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: At the end of the personal assessment, the trainer will draw up a plan, outlining what you should try to achieve within a 6 week period.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This will then be reviewed at the end of the 6 weeks.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, I'll just quickly run through the types of membership we have available.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: All members must pay a joining fee of £90, in addition to the rates for the monthly membership fees.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Gold membership entitles you to free entry at all LP clubs.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There are now LP clubs in all major cities and towns, so if you travel a lot this will be a great advantage.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Individual gold membership costs £50 a month, and joint membership for you and your partner will cost £75.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Premier membership is for professional people whose work commitments make it difficult for them to use the club during the day.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And so LP gives booking preferences to premier members at peak times.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This means you'll find it easier to book the sessions at times that suit you.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Reciprocal arrangements with other LP clubs are available to Premier members.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Premier membership is for individuals only, but you'll be sent passes for guests every month.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The monthly fee is £65.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You don't have to have any special clothes or equipment when you visit the club.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We provide robes and hairdryers in the changing rooms, but it's very important to remember your photo card.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Because you won't be able to get in without it.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: For people who aren't working during the day.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: This starts on day 1 with your personal assessment.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: You are asked to fill in a questionnaire giving details of any health problems.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: One of our personal trainers will then go through this with you.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The trainer will then take you through the safety rules for using the equipment in the fitness suite.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: During your next exercise session, a personal trainer will work with you to make sure you understand these.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It's very important to do this, because we really do want to avoid having any sports injuries.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There's a lot more to looking after yourself than simply lifting weights.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: At the end of the personal assessment, the trainer will draw up a plan, outlining what you should try to achieve within a 6 week period.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: This will then be reviewed at the end of the 6 weeks.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Now, I'll just quickly run through the types of membership we have available.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: All members must pay a joining fee of £90, in addition to the rates for the monthly membership fees.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Gold membership entitles you to free entry at all LP clubs.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There are now LP clubs in all major cities and towns, so if you travel a lot this will be a great advantage.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Individual gold membership costs £50 a month, and joint membership for you and your partner will cost £75.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Premier membership is for professional people whose work commitments make it difficult for them to use the club during the day.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And so LP gives booking preferences to premier members at peak times.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: This means you'll find it easier to book the sessions at times that suit you.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Reciprocal arrangements with other LP clubs are available to Premier members.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Premier membership is for individuals only, but you'll be sent passes for guests every month.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The monthly fee is £65.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: You don't have to have any special clothes or equipment when you visit the club.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We provide robes and hairdryers in the changing rooms, but it's very important to remember your photo card.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Because you won't be able to get in without it.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: For people who aren't working during the day.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

body.title: Which TWO facilities at the leisure club have recently been improved?

items: New members should describe any <input>.
The <input> will be explained to you before you use the equipment.
You will be given a six-week <input>.
items.title: Personal Assessment

items: There is a compulsory £90 <input> fee for members.
Gold members are given <input> to all the LP clubs.
Premier members are given priority during <input> hours.
Premier members can bring some <input> every month.
Members should always take their <input> with them.
items.title: Types of membership

audioscript.text: Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a student called John talking to a professor about a design competition he is entering.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Um, hello, professor.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: I'm John Wishart.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: I'm working on my entry for the global design competition.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: My tutor said you might be able to help me with it.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Oh, yes.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: I got a copy of your drawings.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Uh. Come in and tell me about it.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: What sort of competition is it?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, it's an international design competition, and we have to come up with a new design for a typical domestic kitchen appliance.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: And are there any special conditions? Does it have to save energy for example?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Actually that was the focus in last year's competition.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: This year is different, we have to adopt an innovative approach to existing technology, using it in a way that hasn't been thought of before.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: I see, that sounds tricky.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: And what kitchen appliance have you chosen?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, I decided to choose the dishwasher.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Interesting.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: What made you choose that?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, they're an everyday kitchen appliance in most Australian houses, but they're all pretty boring, and almost identical to each other.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: I think some people will be prepared to pay a little extra for something that looks different.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: It's a nice idea.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: I see you've called your design 'The Rockpool'.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Hmm. Why is that?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Basically because it looks like the rock pools you find on a beach.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: The top is made of glass, so that you can look down into it.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: And there's a stone at the bottom.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Is that just for decoration?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Actually it does have a function. Instead of pushing a button, you turn the stone.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: So it's really just a novel way of starting the dishwasher.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: It's a really nice design, but what makes it innovative?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, I decided to make a dishwasher that uses carbon dioxide.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: In place of water and detergent?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: How will you manage that?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: The idea is to pressurize the carbon dioxide so that it becomes a liquid.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: The fluid is then released into the dishwasher where it cleans the dishes all by itself.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Sounds like a brilliant idea.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Your system will totally do away with the need for strong detergents.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: So what happens once the dishes are clean?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, uh, to allow them to dry, the liquid carbon dioxide and the waste materials all go to an area called the holding chamber.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: That's where the liquid is depressurized, and so it reverts to a gas.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Then the oil and grease are separated out and sent to the waste system.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: It sounds like you've thought it all out very thoroughly.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: So what happens to the carbon dioxide once the process is complete?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Not wasted, I hope.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Actually, that's where the real savings are made.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: The carbon dioxide is sent back to the cylinder, and can be used again and again.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: What a terrific idea.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Do you think it will ever be built?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Hmm. Probably not, but that's OK.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Well, I'm sure a lot of positive things will come out of your design.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now you seem to have thought about everything, so what exactly did you need me to help you with?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, my design has made it to the final stage of the competition, and in a few months' time, I have to give a presentation.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: And that's the part I was hoping you could help me with.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Right, well that should be easy enough.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: What have you managed to do so far?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, I've got detailed drawings to show how it will work, and I've also written a 500-word paper on it.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, if you want to stand a good chance of winning, you really need a model of the machine.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Yes, I thought I might, but I'm having a few problems.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: What's the main difficulty so far?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Let me guess, is it the materials?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Yes, I want it to look professional, but everything that's top quality is also very expensive.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Look, projects like this are very important to us.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: They really help lift our profile.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: So why don't you talk to the university about a grant?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Oh, I can help you fill out the application forms if you like.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Oh, that would be great.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: You'd better show me this paper you've written as well, for a global competition such as this.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: You need to make sure the technical details you've given are accurate and thorough.
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Oh, that would be a great help.
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: Is there anything else I can do?
audioscript.speaker: PROFESSOR

audioscript.text: Well, I'm really...
audioscript.speaker: JOHN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

options: develop a new use for current technology.

The stone in John's 'Rockpool' design is used
In the holding chamber, the carbon dioxide
At the end of the cleaning process, the carbon dioxide

is collected ready to be re-used.

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear a university lecture about an endangered bear in British Columbia, Canada, known as the spirit bear.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Today we continue our series on ecology and conservation with a look at a particularly endangered member of the black bear family.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: One in 10 black bears is actually born with a white coat, which is the result of a special gene that surfaces in a few.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Local people have named it the spirit bear, and according to the legends of these communities, its snowy fur brings with it a special power.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Because of this, it has always been highly regarded by them, so much that they do not speak of seeing it to anyone else.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It is their way of protecting it when strangers visit the area.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The white bear's habitat is quite interesting.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The bear's strong relationship with the old growth rainforest is a complex one.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The white bear relies on the huge centuries old trees in the forest in many ways.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: For example, the old growth trees have extremely long roots that help prevent erosion of the soil along the banks of the many fish streams.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Keeping these banks intact is important, because these streams are home to salmon, which are the bear's main food source.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In return, the bears' feeding habits nurture the forest.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As the bears eat the salmon, they discard the skin and bones in great amounts on the forest floor, which provide vital nutrients.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: These produce lush vegetation that sustains thousands of other types of life forms.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: From birds to insects and more.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Today, the Spirit bear lives off the coast of the province of British Columbia on a few islands.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There is great concern for their survival.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Since it is estimated that less than 200 of these white bears remain.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The best way to protect them is to make every effort to preserve the delicate balance of their forest environment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In other words, their ecosystem.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The greatest threat to the bear's existence is the loss of its habitat.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Over many years, logging companies have stripped the land by cutting down a large number of trees.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In addition, they have built roads which have fractured the areas where the bear usually feeds, and many hibernation sites have also been lost.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The logging of the trees along the streams has damaged the places where the bears fish.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: To make matters worse, the number of salmon in those streams is declining, because there is no legal limit on fishing at the moment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: All these influences have a negative impact on the spirit bear's very existence, which is made all the more fragile by the fact that reproduction among these bears has always been disappointingly low.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And so, what's the situation going forward?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Community organizations, environmental groups, and the British Columbia government are now working together on the problem.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The government is now requiring logging companies to adopt a better logging method, which is a positive step.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: However, these measures alone may not be sufficient to ensure a healthy population of the spirit bear in the future.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Other steps also need to be taken.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: While it is important to maintain the spirit bear's habitat, there also needs to be more emphasis on its expansion.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The move is justified.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As it will also create space for other bears that are losing their homes...
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: In return, the bears' feeding habits nurture the forest.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: As the bears eat the salmon, they discard the skin and bones in great amounts on the forest floor, which provide vital nutrients.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: These produce lush vegetation that sustains thousands of other types of life forms.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: From birds to insects and more.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Today, the Spirit bear lives off the coast of the province of British Columbia on a few islands.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There is great concern for their survival.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Since it is estimated that less than 200 of these white bears remain.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The best way to protect them is to make every effort to preserve the delicate balance of their forest environment.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: In other words, their ecosystem.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The greatest threat to the bear's existence is the loss of its habitat.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Over many years, logging companies have stripped the land by cutting down a large number of trees.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: In addition, they have built roads which have fractured the areas where the bear usually feeds, and many hibernation sites have also been lost.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The logging of the trees along the streams has damaged the places where the bears fish.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: To make matters worse, the number of salmon in those streams is declining, because there is no legal limit on fishing at the moment.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: All these influences have a negative impact on the spirit bear's very existence, which is made all the more fragile by the fact that reproduction among these bears has always been disappointingly low.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And so, what's the situation going forward?
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Community organizations, environmental groups, and the British Columbia government are now working together on the problem.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The government is now requiring logging companies to adopt a better logging method, which is a positive step.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: However, these measures alone may not be sufficient to ensure a healthy population of the spirit bear in the future.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Other steps also need to be taken.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: While it is important to maintain the spirit bear's habitat, there also needs to be more emphasis on its expansion.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The move is justified.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: As it will also create space for other bears that are losing their homes...
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER
